principle: Update parameters along the unit angular bisector of the per-channel unit-normalized score-function gradients, making the update invariant under componentwise positive rescaling of channel rewards.
primitive_type: Policy parameters x trajectory -> (S^{|theta|-1} u {0})^K  (a K-tuple of unit vectors or zeros on the policy parameter sphere)
feedback_signal: info['vector'] per-component vector reward (K=2 channels on DST-concave); per-channel discounted return-to-go computed from vector reward without scalarization
novelty_boundary: CHANBI is NOT a scalarization w^T r: under componentwise positive rescaling r^c <- alpha_c r^c with alpha_c > 0, the per-channel gradient g^c <- alpha_c g^c so the unit gradient ghat_c = g^c/||g^c|| is unchanged, and hence d* = (ghat_1 + ghat_2)/||ghat_1 + ghat_2|| is exactly invariant. No linear or nonlinear scalarization with fixed or adaptive weights has this invariance: any w^T(alpha_1 r^1, alpha_2 r^2) depends on (alpha_1, alpha_2). MGDA (Desideri 2012) uses raw g^c (not normalized) and its direction depends on ||g^c|| ratios, so MGDA is rescaling-sensitive while CHANBI is exactly rescaling-invariant. Nash-MTL (geometric mean of inner products) is also scale-sensitive. CAGrad's constrained QP places d near the average raw gradient, also scale-sensitive. CHANBI's closed-form arithmetic mean of unit gradients is structurally distinct.
empirical_claim: On quick (DST-concave): (a) within ~30 episodes both channels become active (>20% of rollouts have ||g^1||>0); (b) the logged scalar cos_d_uniform = d* . d_unif (where d_unif = (g_1+g_2)/||g_1+g_2||) is strictly < 0.95 in the active-both regime, with mean typically 0.5-0.8, while the ablation has cos_d_uniform == 1 by construction; (c) CHANBI's mean episode length stays > 2 (does not collapse to T=1 like the uniform-sum ablation in run 21); (d) final hypervolume on DST-concave at or above the random floor 194.
falsifier: Primary: cos_d_uniform >= 0.95 on average across the run when both channels are active (operator decorative). Secondary: mean episode length matches ablation within seed variance throughout training (operator behaviorally null). Tertiary: final hypervolume strictly worse than random floor 194 by more than seed variance (operator harmful). Substrate-confounded null: n_active==2 fraction stays at 0 (no treasure found in budget) -- demands non-degenerate vector retest.
ablation_plan: In train_ablate.py, replace the per-channel unit-normalization with the raw sum: skip step 4 (no g_c/||g_c||); set s = g_1 + g_2 directly and update theta <- theta + alpha * s. This recovers REINFORCE on the uniform-weight scalarized return G_t = G^1_t + G^2_t. Preserves all hyperparameters, score-function form, policy architecture, rollout mechanism. Removes only the per-channel norm normalization (the rescaling-invariance). Optional sanity ablation: random sign flip eps_c in {+1, -1} per rollout on each ghat_c.
proof_debt: (1) First-order Pareto-improvement: when ghat_1 . ghat_2 > 0 (acute angle), d* . ghat_c = (1 + ghat_1.ghat_2)/||ghat_1+ghat_2|| > 0 for both c (per-rollout). The expected-improvement theorem E[d* . grad_theta E[G^c]] > 0 along the iterate is open. (2) Convergence under stochastic estimation: standard Robbins-Monro with the bounded step theta <- theta + alpha S d* (S = sum_c ||g^c||); characterize stationary set as Pareto-stationary policies (anti-aligned ghat_1 = -ghat_2 or all ||g^c||=0). (3) Anti-aligned tie-break: characterize when ghat_1 . ghat_2 = -1 occurs on DST-concave and design a tie-break rule that preserves rescaling-invariance. (4) Connection to natural gradient: characterize when d* equals F^{-1} g_avg for some Fisher F (likely only at trivial isotropic-Fisher case). (5) Mirror-descent characterization: identify whether CHANBI is mirror descent on a Bregman geometry over per-channel value functionals.
